experiment: MAGMA Level-2 (A) — fixed-seed harness A/B on ground-truth PNG001, measured through MAGMA's canary oracle
method: Same trigger.png fed to two harnesses linked against MAGMA's SAME canary-instrumented libpng16.a; single-input replay; PNG001 reached/triggered read from MAGMA_STORAGE. Only variable = user_width_max.

arm_A_default.interpretation: width 0x55555555 > 1,000,000 rejected at png_check_IHDR; the vulnerable IDAT chunk-length code is never even REACHED.

arm_B_nemesis.interpretation: raised limit lets the width pass; the row_factor overflow path is reached and the bug condition triggered.

result: Harness augmentation flips PNG001 from NOT-REACHED (0/0) to REACHED+TRIGGERED (1/1) on identical input and library, measured by MAGMA's ground-truth canary. Matches Outcome A: harness construction unlocked an execution state the default harness leaves unreachable.
critical_caveat: MAGMA's build OVERRIDES the stock libpng limit: pnglibconf.h sets PNG_USER_WIDTH_MAX=0x7FFFFFFF (stock is 1,000,000). So against MAGMA's libpng AS SHIPPED, the default harness ALREADY passes the width and BOTH arms trigger (verified: both 1/1). MAGMA neutralised the exact barrier the thesis is about — at the library-config level. To evaluate faithfully to STOCK libpng, Arm A here explicitly restores the stock 1,000,000 default that MAGMA overrode; the variable is then the enforced limit value.
indirect_corroboration: That MAGMA had to raise PNG_USER_WIDTH_MAX to 0x7FFFFFFF to make CVE-2018-13785 benchmarkable independently confirms the default width limit IS the reachability barrier — exactly what the NEMESIS harness augmentation removes at the harness level (without patching the library).

three_oracle_convergence.1_stock_libpng_SIGFPE: benchmarks/harness_reasoning: naive default-limit harness exit 0; +png_set_user_limits SIGFPE (exit 136).
three_oracle_convergence.2_magma_config_inference: MAGMA raised the limit to 0x7FFFFFFF to benchmark the bug.
three_oracle_convergence.3_magma_canary_this_run: Arm A (1M) reached=0/triggered=0; Arm B (max) reached=1/triggered=1.

fully_clean_followup: Recompile MAGMA's libpng16.a with stock PNG_USER_WIDTH_MAX=1000000 so Arm A is the literal UNMODIFIED default harness inheriting the stock default (no explicit call). Expected identical result; removes the last bit of artificiality.
